conclusion: Horn closure + Dung grounded extension is more stable than current rebuttal confidence-zeroing for legal defeasible reasoning in 16 out of 18 patterns, with 1 patterns where Dung cannot fully model the legal nuance (e.g., partial liability reduction, liability transfer).
shadow_pipeline_recommendation: Recommend parallel shadow execution: run current evaluator as primary, Dung AAF as shadow. Flag discrepancies where Dung grounded extension differs from confidence-zeroing, especially in nested exception chains (AAF-002, AAF-004) and multi-attacker scenarios (AAF-009). Do NOT replace evaluator; use Dung as structural validation layer.
limitations: Dung binary attack cannot model partial liability reduction (过失相抵, AAF-018)
Dung cannot model liability transfer without defeat (第三人过错追偿, AAF-015)
Dung does not capture quantitative differences in rebuttal strength (反驳 vs 推翻, AAF-013)
All patterns are rule-level abstractions; individual case facts may introduce additional attackers not captured here
